concept.title: Online and offline projects
concept.shortdesc: A z/OS project is either in online or offline state.

conbody.note: The work offline and work online operations for a remote resource are deprecated.
conbody.p:
A z/OS project is in either of two states:
In online state, the project is connected to the systems to which its subprojects refer. You can directly change the data sets that are stored in those systems.
In offline state, the project can access only mainframe resources that were explicitly taken offline.

When you move a z/OS project to the offline state, you specify the subprojects, data sets, and partitioned data set members to be transferred to the workstation. When you switch back to online state, you select the files you want to upload to the remote system. A window opens that allows for comparison of the local and remote versions of the files. You can use this window to upload or download the versions of the files you would like to keep or to merge changes between local and remote files. Clicking the Work Online button displays a warning message, indicating that any changed files not uploaded to the remote system are lost.
